# Diffusion Policy

## What Diffusion Policy Generates

Diffusion Policy generates short-horizon action trajectories:

$$
\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H}
\sim
p_\theta(\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H} \mid \mathbf{o}_{t-T_o:t})
$$

This gives the policy temporal structure beyond a single next action.

---

## Action Trajectories Instead of Single Actions

- A trajectory can encode approach, contact, and recovery phases.
- Temporal action chunks reduce myopic behavior.
- Multimodal sequence generation can represent several plausible strategies.

TODO: Insert trajectory chunk visualization at `figures/action_trajectory_chunk.png`.

---

## Observation Conditioning

Possible conditioning inputs:

- RGB or RGB-D images
- proprioception
- force/torque measurements
- task labels or language instructions

TODO: Insert observation encoder diagram at `figures/observation_conditioning.png`.

---

## Action Chunking

- Predict $H$ future actions.
- Execute only the first $h \le H$ actions.
- Replan with fresh observations.

This connects diffusion sampling with receding horizon control.

---

## Receding Horizon Execution

At robot time $t$:

1. observe the scene
2. sample an action trajectory
3. execute the first actions
4. discard the remainder
5. replan

TODO: Insert receding horizon timeline at `figures/receding_horizon_execution.png`.

---

## Training Pipeline

- Collect demonstrations.
- Encode observations.
- Add noise to expert action chunks.
- Train the denoising network.

TODO: Insert training pipeline figure at `figures/diffusion_policy_training.png`.

---

## Inference Pipeline

- Encode current observations.
- Initialize noisy action trajectory.
- Iteratively denoise.
- Send selected actions to the controller.

TODO: Insert inference pipeline figure at `figures/diffusion_policy_inference.png`.

---

## Demonstration Video Placeholder

TODO: Insert demo video at `assets/videos/diffusion_policy_demo.mp4`.

```{=html}
<!-- Example future syntax:
<video controls src="assets/videos/diffusion_policy_demo.mp4"></video>
-->
```
